text.title: Google Ads Keyword Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Google Ads Keyword Strategy MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Keywords MUST be tagged by intent (informational / commercial / transactional). Mixed-intent ad groups are rejected.
rule.rationale: Mixed-intent groups cannot share ad copy or landing page; CTR collapses; quality score drops.

rule.statement: Every ad group MUST stack exact + phrase + tight negatives. Broad-only or exact-only ad groups are rejected.
rule.rationale: Broad alone wastes spend; exact alone misses long-tail; stack covers both.

rule.statement: Ad groups MUST cap at 20 keywords. >20-keyword groups are split.
rule.rationale: Above 20 keywords ad copy cannot stay relevant; quality score and CTR drop.

rule.statement: Search Terms Report MUST be mined weekly with named owner. Skipping leaks irrelevant matches into spend.
rule.rationale: STR drift surfaces within days; weekly mining catches it before quarter-end.

rule.statement: Account-level shared negative list MUST exist (brand + competitor + irrelevant). Per-group only is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Shared list ensures negatives apply across campaigns; per-group only leaks at scale.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
